concat.cpp

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 
00008 #include "sprite/tests/test_common.hpp"
00009 #include "sprite/operators.hpp"
00010 #include "sprite/user_module.hpp"
00011 
00015 struct ListModule : sprite::UserModule
00016 {
00017   // Constructor and operation ids.
00018   size_t c_Cons;
00019   size_t c_Nil;
00020   size_t op_concat;
00021 
00023   ListModule(Loader & loader) : UserModule(loader)
00024   {
00025     c_Cons = install_ctor("Cons");
00026     c_Nil = install_ctor("Nil");
00027     op_concat = install_oper("concat", &ListModule::h_concat);
00028   }
00029 
00030   virtual ~ListModule() {}
00031   
00032 private:
00033 
00034   // ====== H function(s) ======
00035   void h_concat(Node & node) const
00036   {
00037     assert(node.arity() == 2);
00038     NodePtr child = node[0];
00039     switch(child->tag())
00040     {
00041       // H.1
00042       case CHOICE: return pull_tab(node, child);
00043       // H.5
00044       case FAIL: return rewrite_fail(node);
00045       // H.2
00046       case OPER: return head_normalize(*child);
00047       // H.3
00048       case CTOR:
00049       {
00050         if(child->id() == c_Nil)
00051           return rewrite_fwd(node, node[1]);
00052         else
00053         {
00054           assert(child->id() == c_Cons);
00055           assert(child->arity() == 2);
00056           rewrite_ctor(
00057               node, c_Cons, (*child)[0]
00058             , Node::create<OPER>(op_concat, (*child)[1], node[1])
00059             );
00060           return;
00061         }
00062       }
00063       default: throw RuntimeError("Type error in concat");
00064     }
00065   }
00066 };
00067 
00069 shared_ptr<Module> load_list(sprite::Loader & loader)
00070   { return boost::make_shared<ListModule>(tr1::ref(loader)); }
00071 
00072 // This is a sample program to demonstrate the use of concat.  The output is
00073 // meant to be inspected visually.
00074 int test_main(int, char**)
00075 {
00076   // ====== DEFINE THE PROGRAM ======
00077 
00078   // Create a new program and make it the context for output.
00079   Program pgm;
00080   std::cout << setprogram(pgm);
00081 
00082   // Import the list module and capture a reference to it.
00083   shared_ptr<Module const> const _list = pgm.import("List");
00084 
00085   // Downcast the list module (alternatively, the abstract Module interface
00086   // could be used).
00087   shared_ptr<ListModule const> const list(
00088       dynamic_pointer_cast<ListModule const>(_list)
00089     );
00090 
00091   // DEBUG - print the linked program.
00092   std::cout << pgm << std::endl;
00093 
00094   // ====== FORM THE GOAL ======
00095 
00096   // Construct this goal: concat [1] [2]
00097   NodePtr lhs = Node::create<CTOR>(
00098       list->c_Cons, Node::create<INT>(1), Node::create<CTOR>(list->c_Nil)
00099     );
00100   NodePtr rhs = Node::create<CTOR>(
00101       list->c_Cons, Node::create<INT>(2), Node::create<CTOR>(list->c_Nil)
00102     );
00103   NodePtr goal = Node::create<OPER>(list->op_concat, lhs, rhs);
00104   std::cout << *goal << std::endl;
00105 
00106   // ====== EXECUTE THE GOAL ======
00107 
00108   execute(pgm, *goal, TRACE);
00109   std::cout << *goal << std::endl;
00110 
00111   return 0;
00112 }
